commit bde6ca6f8be083734e4246bb4ba00e83f69f6583
Author: ddx-checkpoint <checkpoint@ddx.local>
Date:   Sat May 16 19:48:56 2026 -0400

    fix(routing): emit one candidate per tier for multi-tier subscription harnesses [fizeau-8ebac59d]
    
    Under policy=default, the claude subscription harness contributed only its
    DefaultModel candidate even though sibling tiers (sonnet-4.6 alongside
    opus-4.7) shared one auth. The cost-aware scorer never saw the cheaper tier
    because the engine collapsed enumeration to DefaultModel whenever no
    harness pin was supplied.
    
    routingModelsForProvider now expands AutoRoutingModels whenever the request
    leaves Model and Provider unpinned (the harness pin is no longer required),
    so every tier the harness lists becomes its own candidate keyed on
    (harness, model).
    
    ProviderEntry gains CostUSDPer1kTokensByModel; buildHarnessCandidates uses
    it to attach a per-tier cost to each candidate emitted under the same
    provider. Service-side, applySubscriptionRoutingCost projects catalog cost
    per AutoRoutingModels entry into that map, and buildRoutingInputsWithCatalog
    overrides AutoRoutingModels/SupportedModels with the active concrete tier
    IDs the catalog publishes for the harness surface so subscription harnesses
    expose every catalog-known tier instead of the alias-collapsed set.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

data.text:
All four ACs are already satisfied in the current tree by prior commit `bde6ca6f` (fizeau-8ebac59d):

- AC1 (per-tier enumeration): `internal/routing/engine.go:1539-1551` enumerates `AutoRoutingModels` when `req.Model == "" && req.Provider == ""`; verified by `TestAutoRouteEnumeratesPerTierForMultiTierHarness` in `internal/routing/engine_test.go`.
- AC2 (per-tier cost): `ProviderEntry.CostUSDPer1kTokensByModel` is populated by `applySubscriptionRoutingCost` (`service_routing.go:1628-1655`) via `subscriptionCostByModel`, and the engine attaches it per-candidate at `internal/routing/engine.go:1456-1468`; verified by `TestPerTierCandidateCarriesCatalogPowerAndCost` (asserts opus=0.045 vs sonnet=0.009 — distinct per-tier costs sourced from the per-model map, not DefaultModel).
- AC3/AC4: `go test ./...` is green; routing-package and service-package tests pass.

Writing a `no_changes_rationale.txt` with a `verification_command` so DDx can re-verify the ACs are satisfied.

input.content:
verification_command: go test -run "TestAutoRouteEnumeratesPerTierForMultiTierHarness|TestPerTierCandidateCarriesCatalogPowerAndCost|TestPerTierCandidateRoutesViaSameHarness" ./internal/routing/

All acceptance criteria for fizeau-f7fa1d87 are already satisfied in the tree at
base-rev 052dcbaf by prior commit bde6ca6f (fizeau-8ebac59d "fix(routing): emit
one candidate per tier for multi-tier subscription harnesses"). No additional
changes are required.

Evidence mapping
================

AC1 — Under policy=default with no --harness/--model pin and a multi-tier
claude HarnessEntry, routing.Resolve emits >1 candidate for that harness in
Decision.Candidates.

  Implementation: internal/routing/engine.go:1539-1551
    routingModelsForProvider returns append([]string(nil), h.AutoRoutingModels...)
    whenever req.Model == "" && req.Provider == "" && len(h.AutoRoutingModels) > 0.
    The original guard (req.Harness != "") that collapsed unpinned dispatches to
    DefaultModel is gone; the harness pin is no longer required for enumeration.

  Test: internal/routing/engine_test.go TestAutoRouteEnumeratesPerTierForMultiTierHarness
    Builds a multi-tier claude subscription fixture (AutoRoutingModels =
    {opus-4.7, sonnet-4.6}), calls Resolve(Request{Policy: "default"}, in), and
    asserts both opus-4.7 and sonnet-4.6 appear in dec.Candidates under
    Harness == "claude".

AC2 — Each per-tier candidate carries the correct cost from its catalog entry,
not the DefaultModel cost. Verified by asserting Candidate.CostUSDPer1kTokens
differs across tiers in the test.

  Implementation:
    - routing.ProviderEntry gains CostUSDPer1kTokensByModel
      (internal/routing/engine.go around the ProviderEntry struct).
    - buildHarnessCandidates uses the per-model map to attach a per-tier cost
      to each candidate emitted under the same provider
      (internal/routing/engine.go:1456-1468: `if perModel, ok :=
      p.CostUSDPer1kTokensByModel[model]; ok { candidateCost = perModel }`).
    - service_routing.go:1628-1655 applySubscriptionRoutingCost projects
      catalog cost per AutoRoutingModels entry into CostUSDPer1kTokensByModel
      via subscriptionCostByModel (service_routing.go:1661-1679).

  Test: internal/routing/engine_test.go TestPerTierCandidateCarriesCatalogPowerAndCost
    Fixture supplies CostUSDPer1kTokensByModel = {opus-4.7: 0.045, sonnet-4.6: 0.009}.
    Test asserts Candidate.CostUSDPer1kTokens == 0.045 for opus-4.7 and == 0.009
    for sonnet-4.6 (i.e. each per-tier candidate's cost matches its own catalog
    entry, distinct from sibling tiers and distinct from any single DefaultModel
    cost). It also asserts CostSource == CostSourceSubscription per tier.

  Supplementary test: service_routing_test.go TestSubscriptionRoutingCostPopulatesPerTierCatalogCost
    Covers the service-side projection from catalog into
    CostUSDPer1kTokensByModel.

  Note on the "~3.0 / ~15.0 per Mtok" guidance in the AC text: the engine-level
  test uses synthetic fixture costs (0.045 vs 0.009 USD/1k tok) chosen to make
  the per-tier-distinctness invariant unambiguous. The load-bearing assertion
  the AC names — "Candidate.CostUSDPer1kTokens differs across tiers" — is
  satisfied. Production catalog values flow through unchanged via
  subscriptionCostByModel/catalogCostUSDPer1kTokens.

AC3 — go test ./internal/routing/... ./... green.

  Verified in this worktree: ran `go test ./...` to completion; all packages
  report ok (including internal/routing, fizeau root package, harness suites,
  serviceimpl, etc.). No failures, no skips that mask the new code paths.

AC4 — No regression in service_adr005_test.go, service_routing_test.go,
internal/routing/engine_test.go.

  Verified: the named test files compile and pass within the full `go test ./...`
  run above; the three named per-tier tests in engine_test.go pass under a
  targeted run (verification_command above re-asserts this).

Why no commit
=============

The bead is a duplicate-by-scope of fizeau-8ebac59d, which already landed both
the engine change (enumerate AutoRoutingModels without requiring a harness pin)
and the per-tier cost plumbing (ProviderEntry.CostUSDPer1kTokensByModel +
subscriptionCostByModel + per-candidate cost selection in buildHarnessCandidates),
along with the unit tests the AC names. Re-implementing or re-asserting any of
this would either duplicate existing tests or churn already-correct code.

data.text:
All ACs for `fizeau-f7fa1d87` are already satisfied by prior commit `bde6ca6f` (fizeau-8ebac59d). The engine enumerates `AutoRoutingModels` without requiring a harness pin (`internal/routing/engine.go:1539-1551`), and per-tier cost is plumbed via `ProviderEntry.CostUSDPer1kTokensByModel` + `subscriptionCostByModel` + per-candidate cost selection. The three named per-tier tests in `internal/routing/engine_test.go` pass, and `go test ./...` is green.

Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` with a `verification_command` that re-runs the three per-tier tests; exit 0 closes the bead.
